primary_findings.finding: Temporal masked-diffusion recipe surface runs end-to-end with video pretraining, calibration, W&B, and H200 evidence.
Model collapses to frequent small mouse-motion tokens and almost never emits semantically correct key/button events.
primary_findings.evidence: top predicted tokens [('MOUSE_DY_Z0', 5000), ('MOUSE_DX_N2', 4992), ('MOUSE_DX_N1', 4992), ('MOUSE_DX_N3', 2955), ('MOUSE_DY_P1', 2034), ('MOUSE_DY_N1', 10), ('KEY_PRESS_65', 8), ('KEY_RELEASE_65', 8)]; keyboard=0; button F1=0; diagnostic predicted button examples=0

primary_findings.finding: Single global non-noop budget is not sufficient: calibration selected many non-noop tokens but objective score stayed zero.
Post-training prediction phase is GPU-underutilized and CPU/Python-loop heavy; future full-corpus runs need batched/vectorized inference and/or larger batch before 4xH200 promotion.

recommended_next_branch.name: family-budgeted temporal masked-diffusion IDM
recommended_next_branch.recipe_fidelity: Preserve FDM-1 public IDM shape; change only split-safe iterative unmasking/calibration to allocate separate key/button/mouse budgets instead of a single non-noop threshold.
implementation_notes: family-specific candidate extraction from center-token probabilities
held-out-train calibration for keyboard/button/mouse families with button FPR cap
prediction summary records family budgets and no target-label calibration

checkpoint_policy: Do not checkpoint G005; continue exploration under recipe-alignment gate.
